articles.why_it_matters: Emphasizes the military's proactive stance on AI threats in cybersecurity.

articles.title: OpenAI president says AI is now writing 80% of the company’s code

articles.title: The man who built Internet Explorer wants to teach AI to think on 20 watts
articles.snippet: Thomas Reardon has a pattern. In 1994, he created the project that became Internet Explorer, the browser that turned Microsoft from a software company into an internet company and triggered the most consequential antitrust case in technology history. In 2015,…
articles.ai_summary: Thomas Reardon, the creator of Internet Explorer, is now focused on developing AI that operates efficiently on just 20 watts, aiming to enhance AI accessibility and sustainability.
articles.why_it_matters: Innovates AI efficiency, potentially transforming how AI systems are deployed in various environments.

articles.title: Community 'disappointed' by Sheraton high-rise approval
articles.snippet: Activists appealed the local council's approval of the 18-storey resort on the grounds that it would endanger local wildlife. The community fears the court's green light for the development will permanently change their seaside town.
articles.ai_summary: Local activists expressed disappointment over the approval of an 18-storey Sheraton resort, arguing it threatens local wildlife and could alter the community's character.
articles.why_it_matters: Highlights ongoing tensions between development and environmental conservation in local communities.

articles.snippet: Buried on page nine of Microsoft’s 10-Q for the quarter ended March 31, 2026 is a paragraph worthy of attention. Why? What does it reveal? A lot. For starters, Microsoft now holds approximately 27 percent of OpenAI on an as-converted basis, accounted for unde…
articles.ai_summary: Microsoft's recent 10-Q filing reveals it holds about 27% of OpenAI, indicating a significant investment and influence in the AI sector.
